international-outdoor-standards.pdf.title: Laser Safety Concepts for Aviation
international-outdoor-standards.pdf.author: Patrick Murphy, LaserPointerSafety.com
key_topics: FAA and UK CAA incident statistics
international-outdoor-standards.pdf.summary: Comprehensive review of laser safety concepts for pilots and aviation officials. Primary hazard is visual interference during critical flight phases, not eye injury. Zero documented permanent eye injuries in 20,000+ incidents. Covers distraction, glare, flashblindness effects; NOHD calculations with safety factors; pilot protective eyewear recommendations; and legal consequences of aiming lasers at aircraft.

securing-outdoor-shows.pdf.title: FAA Advisory Circular 70-2A
securing-outdoor-shows.pdf.author: U.S. Department of Transportation, FAA
key_topics: Reporting of laser illumination of aircraft
securing-outdoor-shows.pdf.summary: FAA AC 70-2A provides guidance on reporting unauthorized laser illumination incidents. ATC treats laser incidents as in-flight emergencies. Details reporting procedures through DEN, ATC broadcast protocols (every 5 minutes for 20 minutes), ATIS broadcasts for 1 hour, and aircrew mitigation procedures including completing the Laser Beam Exposure Questionnaire.

outdoor-hazard-calculations.pdf.title: FAA Advisory Circular 70-1B
outdoor-hazard-calculations.pdf.author: U.S. Department of Transportation, FAA
key_topics: Flight zone classifications (LFZ, CFZ, SFZ, NFZ)
Coordination with FAA for outdoor shows
outdoor-hazard-calculations.pdf.summary: Comprehensive FAA guidance for planning outdoor laser operations affecting the National Airspace System. Defines four flight zones: Laser Free Zone (LFZ <2NM, <2000ft, <50 μW/cm²), Critical Flight Zone (CFZ), Sensitive Flight Zone (SFZ), and Normal Flight Zone (NFZ). Provides calculation methods for visual interference hazards and coordination requirements with FAA.

notam-procedures.pdf.title: FAA Form 7140-1
notam-procedures.pdf.author: U.S. Department of Transportation, FAA
key_topics: Notice of Proposed Outdoor Laser Operation
notam-procedures.pdf.summary: FAA Form 7140-1 for notifying the FAA of proposed outdoor laser operations. Required for any outdoor laser operation that may affect navigable airspace. Form captures laser specifications, operation details, hazard calculations, and contact information for coordination with FAA Air Traffic.
